senators.name: Jerry Moran
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in economics from University of Kansas and J.D. from University of Kansas School of Law. Career as attorney and state senator. Long-tenured senator with expertise on veterans affairs and agriculture. Solid policy knowledge but more legislative dealmaker than analytical policy wonk.
education: J.D., University of Kansas School of Law

senators.name: Roger Marshall
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in biochemistry from Kansas State University and M.D. from University of Kansas School of Medicine. Practiced as an OB/GYN for decades, delivering over 5,000 babies. Biochemistry degree plus medical degree provides deep scientific foundation. Applies clinical medical expertise to healthcare and public health policy in the Senate. Army Reserve Captain.
education: M.D., University of Kansas School of Medicine

KS-01.name: Tracey Mann
KS-01.notNerdReason: B.S. in agricultural economics from Kansas State University. Career in farming and real estate. Agricultural economics degree provides some quantitative foundation but legislative approach is not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.
education: B.S. Agricultural Economics, Kansas State University

KS-02.name: Derek Schmidt
KS-02.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Kansas, M.A. in international politics from University of Leicester, J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. Three post-secondary degrees including an international master's and elite law degree. 12 years as Kansas Attorney General. Career demonstrates sustained intellectual rigor across law, international affairs, and public policy.
education: M.A. International Politics, University of Leicester

KS-03.name: Sharice Davids
KS-03.nerdReason: A.A. from Johnson County Community College, B.B.A. from University of Missouri-Kansas City, and J.D. from Cornell Law School. First-generation college student who earned an elite law degree. White House Fellow at the Department of Transportation. Career combines legal analysis, economic development on tribal lands, and transportation policy expertise. First openly LGBT Native American elected to Congress.
education: A.A. Liberal Arts, Johnson County Community College
experience: White House Fellow, Dept. of Transportation
Director, Economic Development, Red Cloud Indian School

KS-04.name: Ron Estes
KS-04.nerdReason: B.S. in civil engineering and M.B.A. from Tennessee Technological University. Career as engineer across aerospace, oil and gas, automotive, and manufacturing sectors, working at firms including Andersen Consulting, Koch Industries, and Bombardier Learjet. One of the few engineers in Congress. Served as Kansas State Treasurer. Engineering background provides strong analytical and quantitative foundation.
education: B.S. Civil Engineering, Tennessee Technological University
experience: Worked at Koch Industries, Bombardier Learjet
